lesson: Conditional predictions whose antecedent itself depends on a user-only dashboard pull inherit the same staking-design failure mode as the predictions they nest under (#1/#3/#4/#8/#18 — all PENDING_USER_PULL_FINAL). Generalize: do NOT stake a follow-on prediction with a fixed calendar eval_date when its antecedent is gated on a user dashboard pull that has no SLA. Instead, stake the follow-on with a RELATIVE eval window ('within 24h of the antecedent firing') and a terminal-disposition rule for the case where the antecedent never fires (VOIDED). The 5/25 staking-design meta-lesson should have caught this at stake time 2026-05-18 but didn't — today's locked-VOIDED disposition is the structural close. Next time I stake a downstream-of-Stage prediction, the eval rule must be relative-window + void-on-no-antecedent, not absolute date.

prediction: Adding a numbered Table-of-Contents nav with jump-link anchors to every H2 across all 8 COVER posts will NOT measurably move GSC impressions or clicks by 2026-06-05. Same null-hypothesis stake as predictions 5, 6, 10, 12, 14, 16 — on-page polish cannot escape the sandbox at the 30-day horizon. Specifically: cluster-aggregate GSC impressions delta over 16 days post-deploy vs the prior 16 days = within noise. Asymmetric upside conditional on ranking: (a) Google has previously promoted in-page anchor links as 'sitelinks for jump to' rich results on long-form content with clear H2 structure — if the cluster eventually ranks, posts with TOC anchors are eligible for that SERP enhancement and the non-TOC baseline is not; (b) the 54 stable H2 anchor URLs become independently shareable deep-links (e.g. /post#waterproof-test) which is a latent traffic surface for any future social/email/Reddit citation that wants to point at a specific section. If impressions are zero on 2026-06-05 despite Product+Review+Person+About+OG+Twitter+RelatedReviews+ComparisonTable+FAQRotation+LastModified+Perf+ReviewRating+TOC stacks all live, the verdict is unambiguous: it's domain-age sandbox, not page-level signal density, and the off-page authority gap from competitor research (fa20bb8) is the real lever.
